title: ITSI Episode Volume, Acknowledgement Latency, MTTR, and Auto-Close Effectiveness Monitoring (hourly multisearch outcome analytics, on-call and ServiceNow handoff, NEAP noise and merge integrity, subgroup MTTR anomaly detection)

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: required — Event Analytics, NEAP, Episode Review, grouped and tracked alert indexes

dataSources: index=itsi_grouped_alerts (episode lifecycle, status, severity_label, owner, smart grouping context), index=itsi_tracked_alerts (notable creation feeding episodes, KPI and correlation identifiers for repeat detection), Key Value lookup itsi_event_management_audit or index=_audit (acknowledgement, resolve, severity_change audit), index=oncall or index=main with sourcetype=splunk_oncall:incident and sourcetype=pagerduty:incident (escalation latency, routing fidelity), index=snow sourcetype=snow:incident (formal MTTR and assignment groups), lookup itsi_service_inventory.csv (service, owner_team, business_tier, on_call_team, runbook_url), index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory (environment enrichment for baselines)
app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) plus Splunk On-Call or PagerDuty ingestion and optional ServiceNow ITOM connector
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.3 ITSI episode outcome MTTA MTTR autoclose on-call snow audit KV multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,service_title,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,owner,squad,"unowned")))
      | eval episode_id=trim(toString(coalesce(episode_id,group_id,episodeid,event_id,"")))
      | eval severity_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity,severity_label,"unknown"))))
      | eval st=lower(trim(toString(status)))
      | eval auto_close_hit=if(match(st,"clos") AND match(_raw,"(?i)auto|policy|aggregation|rule"),1,0)
      | eval manual_close_hit=if(match(st,"clos") AND auto_close_hit==0,1,0)
      | eval zero_action_ep=if(match(st,"clos") AND NOT match(st,"ack|progress|assign|work|review"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats count AS ep_lines dc(episode_id) AS ep_dc sum(auto_close_hit) AS auto_sum sum(manual_close_hit) AS man_sum sum(zero_action_ep) AS zsum BY _time service owner_team
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(ep_lines,3)
      | eval mtta_min=0.0
      | eval mttr_min=round(if(ep_dc>0,(ep_lines*2.5)/ep_dc,0),2)
      | eval autoclose_pct=if(ep_lines>0,round(100*auto_sum/ep_lines,2),0)
      | eval manualclose_pct=if(ep_lines>0,round(100*man_sum/ep_lines,2),0)
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=if(ep_lines>0,round(zsum/ep_lines,4),0)
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_grouped" ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpi,kpi_name,"na"))))
      | eval episode_key=trim(toString(coalesce(episode_id,drill_id,"unk_ep")))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats dc(kpi_key) AS kdc count AS tcnt BY _time service episode_key
      | eval rep_flag=if(kdc>1,1,0)
      | stats sum(tcnt) AS tracked_tot sum(rep_flag) AS rep_cnt BY _time service
      | eval owner_team="tracked_unassigned"
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(tracked_tot/4,3)
      | eval mtta_min=0.0
      | eval mttr_min=0.0
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=if(tracked_tot>0,round(rep_cnt/tracked_tot,4),0)
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=round(if(tracked_tot>0,rep_cnt/tracked_tot,0),4)
      | eval lane="arm_tracked" ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_event_management_audit
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_key,target_service,"")))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,actor_team,"audit_team")))
      | eval open_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(open_time,created_time,"1970-01-01T00:00:00"))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
      | eval ack_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(ack_time,acknowledged_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
      | eval res_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(resolve_time,resolved_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
      | eval _time=coalesce(open_ts,now())
      | eval mtta_min=round(if(isnotnull(ack_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts) AND ack_ts>=open_ts,(ack_ts-open_ts)/60,0),2)
      | eval mttr_min=round(if(isnotnull(res_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts) AND res_ts>=open_ts,(res_ts-open_ts)/60,0),2)
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats avg(mtta_min) AS mtta_min avg(mttr_min) AS mttr_min count AS aud_n BY _time service owner_team
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(aud_n,3)
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=0.0
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_audit_kv" ]
    [ search (index=oncall OR index=main) earliest=-24h@h latest=now (sourcetype=splunk_oncall:incident OR sourcetype=pagerduty:incident)
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,routing_key,entity_id,service_id,"oncall_svc")))
      | eval owner_team="oncall_routing"
      | eval page_latency_sec=tonumber(coalesce(time_to_ack_seconds,seconds_to_first_page,time_to_ack,90))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats perc95(page_latency_sec) AS p95sec count AS oc_n BY _time service owner_team
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=round(p95sec/60,2)
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(oc_n,3)
      | eval mtta_min=round(p95sec/60,2)
      | eval mttr_min=0.0
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=0.0
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_oncall_pd" ]
    [ search index=snow earliest=-24h@h latest=now sourcetype=snow:incident
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(u_service,u_cmdb_ci,business_service,"snow_svc")))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(assignment_group,"snow_queue")))
      | eval create_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(sys_created_on,opened_at,"1970-01-01 00:00:00"))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval resolve_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(resolved_at,closed_at,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval _time=coalesce(create_ts,now())
      | eval mttr_min=round(if(isnotnull(resolve_ts) AND isnotnull(create_ts) AND resolve_ts>=create_ts,(resolve_ts-create_ts)/60,0),2)
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats avg(mttr_min) AS mttr_min count AS snow_n BY _time service owner_team
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(snow_n,3)
      | eval mtta_min=0.0
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=0.0
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_snow" ]
| eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unspecified_service")))
| eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_team")))
| stats max(episodes_per_hour) AS episodes_per_hour max(mtta_min) AS mtta_min max(mttr_min) AS mttr_min max(autoclose_pct) AS autoclose_pct max(manualclose_pct) AS manualclose_pct max(escalation_latency_p95_min) AS escalation_latency_p95_min max(noise_ratio) AS noise_ratio max(repeated_episode_rate) AS repeated_episode_rate values(lane) AS lane_vals BY _time service owner_team
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv
     | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_title,"")))
     | eval owner_team_inv=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,"platform_ops")))
     | eval business_tier=trim(toString(coalesce(business_tier,tier,"standard")))
     | eval on_call_team=trim(toString(coalesce(on_call_team,pager_office,"primary_rotation")))
     | eval runbook_url=trim(toString(coalesce(runbook_url,"https://runbooks.example/itsi_episodes")))
     | fields service owner_team_inv business_tier on_call_team runbook_url ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team_inv,owner_team,"unowned_team")
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,ci_name,"")))
     | dedup service
     | eval environment=trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"prod")))
     | fields service environment ]
| eval business_tier=coalesce(business_tier,"standard")
| eval on_call_team=coalesce(on_call_team,owner_team)
| sort 0 service - _time
| streamstats window=168 current=t global=f avg(mttr_min) AS mttr_7d_avg avg(mtta_min) AS mtta_7d_avg BY service owner_team
| eventstats perc95(mttr_min) AS mttr_env_p95 BY environment
| eval subgroup_mttr_anomaly=if(mttr_min>(coalesce(mttr_env_p95,0)*1.35) OR mttr_min>coalesce(mttr_7d_avg*1.5,999999),1,0)
| eval severity=case(coalesce(escalation_latency_p95_min,0)>15 OR coalesce(mttr_env_p95,0)>60 OR (coalesce(autoclose_pct,0)>40 AND coalesce(manualclose_pct,0)<12 AND subgroup_mttr_anomaly==1),"critical", coalesce(subgroup_mttr_anomaly,0)==1 OR coalesce(mttr_min,0)>60,"high", coalesce(autoclose_pct,0)>40,"high", coalesce(noise_ratio,0)>0.35 OR coalesce(repeated_episode_rate,0)>0.25,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","page_service_owner_review_neap_autoclose_escalation_ladder_audit_kv_and_ticketing_fidelity", severity=="high","triage_merged_episodes_suppression_rules_severity_promotion_and_api_throttle_illusion", severity=="medium","tune_smart_group_similarity_on_call_routing_and_service_tag_governance", true(),"continue_weekly_outcome_review_sliding_7d_and_sustained_mttr_retro")
| table _time, service, owner_team, business_tier, on_call_team, episodes_per_hour, mtta_min, mttr_min, autoclose_pct, manualclose_pct, escalation_latency_p95_min, noise_ratio, severity, recommended_action
description: Detects operational effectiveness risks on Splunk ITSI episodes by correlating hourly grouped alert volume, tracked-alert repeat patterns, audit-derived acknowledgement and resolution minutes, Splunk On-Call and PagerDuty escalation latency, and ServiceNow incident resolution timing, then flagging subgroup MTTR anomalies against environment baselines and seven-day sliding averages.
value: Reliability leaders see whether incident response is truly fast or only looks fast because auto-close and ticketing skew hide customer pain, while episode managers prove routing fidelity and noise discipline with evidence that spans ITSI, on-call tools, and ServiceNow rather than a single pane guess.
grandmaExplanation: We time how fast a busy emergency department admits, labels urgency, starts treatment, and discharges people, compared with how long patients still look sick in the waiting room. When the paperwork says everyone was helped quickly but the waiting room tells a different story, we surface that gap so leaders fix triage and handoffs instead of trusting the headline alone.
implementation: Schedule uc_13_2_3_itsi_episode_outcome_effectiveness hourly with earliest=-24h@h on the ITSI search head; forward itsi_grouped_alerts, itsi_tracked_alerts, on-call and PagerDuty incidents, ServiceNow incidents, and materialize itsi_event_management_audit; maintain itsi_service_inventory.csv and cmdb service_inventory; page on severity critical or high with four-hour dedupe; route by owner_team with Episode Review and ticketing drill links.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with episode managers who govern Notable Event Aggregation Policy objects, service owners who maintain Splunk IT Service Intelligence service blueprints, and incident commanders who operate Splunk On-Call or PagerDuty rotations alongside ServiceNow ITOM workflows. This use case measures human-process operational effectiveness on grouped episodes: how fast teams acknowledge, how long resolution takes, whether auto-close policies create false closure that flatters mean time to resolve, how well on-call routing matches service ownership, and whether suppression rules hide real pain or inflate noise. It is deliberately distinct from UC-13.2.1, which trends composite Service Health Score regression against episode pressure; from UC-13.2.2, which emphasizes per-KPI degradation burn-rate and threshold crossings; and from UC-13.2.6, which monitors the rules engine throughput, correlation scheduler skips, and Key Value store latency behind aggregation. When those siblings are healthy but customer trust erodes, the gap is often here in acknowledgement discipline, escalation ladder fidelity, ticketing handoff delays, and mis-tuned episode closure policies.

You must understand episode lifecycle language in production: open or new work that has not been triaged, in-progress states after an owner engages, acknowledgement timestamps that prove someone took ownership, resolved states after mitigating customer impact, and closed states after hygiene and documentation complete. In Event Analytics, Notable Event Aggregation Policy smart grouping decides which raw notables collapse into one episode object; the smart group field and similarity thresholds determine whether analysts see one coherent story or a spray of singletons. Severity reset on severity_change actions can re-open executive attention after an incorrect initial classification; if promotion rules misfire, escalation latency looks artificially short because the clock started on the wrong tier. Auto-close rules and time-bounded closure actions can bias MTTR downward when episodes are marked closed while customer-visible defects continue; pairing auto-close percentage with manual-close percentage and with external on-call latency exposes that illusion. Suppression rules and maintenance windows can zero out episode creation while KPIs still scream; integrity checks between itsi_tracked_alerts volume and itsi_grouped_alerts episode materialization catch those mismatches early.

Splunk On-Call, PagerDuty, and ServiceNow each carry part of the story. On-call tools expose time-to-page and ladder steps; ServiceNow carries formal incident clocks and assignment groups; ITSI carries the episode queue. A throttled ticketing application programming interface can leave ITSI closed while the ticket is still new, producing MTTR that looks excellent in one system and awful in another. Cross-region deployments must align time zones on hourly bins or acknowledgement latency appears worse for teams whose business hours lag coordinated universal time windows. Key Value collections backing audit lookups can add search latency when collections grow; sample audit lookups off-peak and size search concurrency appropriately.

Role-based access for Episode Review, aggregation policy editors, and audit lookups typically requires elevated ITSI roles plus read access to on-call and ticketing indexes. Store automation credentials in the secret store and rotate them with platform policy. Legal reviewers may require masking of assignment group names that embed customer identifiers; keep enrichment in governed lookups rather than free-text logs when possible.

Risk briefing: this alert can embarrass teams when metrics surface cultural problems rather than technical faults. Pair outputs with blameless post-incident review cadences and executive dashboards that emphasize trend improvement, not single-shift shaming. Planned drills and game days must carry metadata tags so outcome metrics exclude rehearsal traffic after governance review.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.2.3 is outcome and operational effectiveness on episodes, not service health curves, not raw KPI threshold burn, and not rules-engine internals in isolation. It answers whether the organization resolves grouped incidents fast enough, honestly enough, and quietly enough relative to noise.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane grouped ingests index=itsi_grouped_alerts with episode identifiers, owner, status, severity labels, and raw text that may reveal auto-close policy verbs. Normalize service fields early and retain event_id or episode_id keys for deduplication. Flatten nested JSON at ingest if forwarders emit multiline episodes so dc(episode_id) remains trustworthy.

Lane tracked ingests index=itsi_tracked_alerts for notable creation feeding episodes. Retain kpi identifiers and correlation metadata when present. Compare rolling counts to grouped episodes; sustained divergence suggests aggregation policy mismatch, suppression, or rules-engine saturation best handled in UC-13.2.6 but visible here as integrity risk.

Lane audit_kv reads Key Value export itsi_event_management_audit when your team materialises acknowledgement and resolution timestamps for governance. If the lookup is absent, mirror the same schema from index=_audit with action patterns tied to episode lifecycle changes and document field mappings in props.conf. Refresh the collection after upgrades so severity_change rows remain visible.

Lane oncall_pd reads Splunk On-Call incidents from sourcetype splunk_oncall:incident and PagerDuty incidents from sourcetype pagerduty:incident on index=oncall or index=main depending on your ingestion contract. Capture time_to_ack style seconds and routing keys that align to service identifiers used in ITSI.

Lane snow reads ServiceNow incidents on index=snow with sourcetype snow:incident for resolved_at and assignment_group fields that validate MTTR against the official record of work.

Maintain lookup itsi_service_inventory.csv with service, owner_team, business_tier, on_call_team, and runbook_url columns refreshed from configuration management. Maintain index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory as a deduped configuration management database export keyed by service for environment tags used in eventstats baselines.

props.conf should preserve _time fidelity on alert indexes. Retention must cover full weeks for seven-day sliding windows in streamstats. Forward _internal from search heads only when required for supplemental audits; do not duplicate massive internal volume without storage review.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_3_itsi_episode_outcome_effectiveness with an hourly schedule in steady state, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate service and owner_team pairs for four hours unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies with deep links to Episode Review, aggregation policy editors, Splunk On-Call schedules, and ServiceNow assignment groups.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans grouped, tracked, audit Key Value, on-call and PagerDuty, and ServiceNow arms so one silent lane cannot hide broken handoffs. stats merges hourly bins per service and owner_team before join enriches inventory and cmdb environment. streamstats computes seven-day sliding averages of mttr_min and mtta_min per service and owner_team using window=168 on hourly rows. eventstats derives environment percentile baselines for subgroup MTTR anomaly detection. case maps escalation latency, auto-close dominance, noise_ratio from zero-action closures, and repeated_episode_rate from tracked duplicates into severity. recommended_action steers owners toward NEAP auto-close review, suppression tuning, merge analysis, ladder fixes, or ticketing throughput problems.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.3 ITSI episode outcome MTTA MTTR autoclose on-call snow audit KV multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,service_title,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,owner,squad,"unowned")))
      | eval episode_id=trim(toString(coalesce(episode_id,group_id,episodeid,event_id,"")))
      | eval severity_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity,severity_label,"unknown"))))
      | eval st=lower(trim(toString(status)))
      | eval auto_close_hit=if(match(st,"clos") AND match(_raw,"(?i)auto|policy|aggregation|rule"),1,0)
      | eval manual_close_hit=if(match(st,"clos") AND auto_close_hit==0,1,0)
      | eval zero_action_ep=if(match(st,"clos") AND NOT match(st,"ack|progress|assign|work|review"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats count AS ep_lines dc(episode_id) AS ep_dc sum(auto_close_hit) AS auto_sum sum(manual_close_hit) AS man_sum sum(zero_action_ep) AS zsum BY _time service owner_team
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(ep_lines,3)
      | eval mtta_min=0.0
      | eval mttr_min=round(if(ep_dc>0,(ep_lines*2.5)/ep_dc,0),2)
      | eval autoclose_pct=if(ep_lines>0,round(100*auto_sum/ep_lines,2),0)
      | eval manualclose_pct=if(ep_lines>0,round(100*man_sum/ep_lines,2),0)
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=if(ep_lines>0,round(zsum/ep_lines,4),0)
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_grouped" ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpi,kpi_name,"na"))))
      | eval episode_key=trim(toString(coalesce(episode_id,drill_id,"unk_ep")))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats dc(kpi_key) AS kdc count AS tcnt BY _time service episode_key
      | eval rep_flag=if(kdc>1,1,0)
      | stats sum(tcnt) AS tracked_tot sum(rep_flag) AS rep_cnt BY _time service
      | eval owner_team="tracked_unassigned"
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(tracked_tot/4,3)
      | eval mtta_min=0.0
      | eval mttr_min=0.0
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=if(tracked_tot>0,round(rep_cnt/tracked_tot,4),0)
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=round(if(tracked_tot>0,rep_cnt/tracked_tot,0),4)
      | eval lane="arm_tracked" ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_event_management_audit
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_key,target_service,"")))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,actor_team,"audit_team")))
      | eval open_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(open_time,created_time,"1970-01-01T00:00:00"))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
      | eval ack_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(ack_time,acknowledged_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
      | eval res_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(resolve_time,resolved_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
      | eval _time=coalesce(open_ts,now())
      | eval mtta_min=round(if(isnotnull(ack_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts) AND ack_ts>=open_ts,(ack_ts-open_ts)/60,0),2)
      | eval mttr_min=round(if(isnotnull(res_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts) AND res_ts>=open_ts,(res_ts-open_ts)/60,0),2)
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats avg(mtta_min) AS mtta_min avg(mttr_min) AS mttr_min count AS aud_n BY _time service owner_team
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(aud_n,3)
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=0.0
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_audit_kv" ]
    [ search (index=oncall OR index=main) earliest=-24h@h latest=now (sourcetype=splunk_oncall:incident OR sourcetype=pagerduty:incident)
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,routing_key,entity_id,service_id,"oncall_svc")))
      | eval owner_team="oncall_routing"
      | eval page_latency_sec=tonumber(coalesce(time_to_ack_seconds,seconds_to_first_page,time_to_ack,90))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats perc95(page_latency_sec) AS p95sec count AS oc_n BY _time service owner_team
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=round(p95sec/60,2)
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(oc_n,3)
      | eval mtta_min=round(p95sec/60,2)
      | eval mttr_min=0.0
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=0.0
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_oncall_pd" ]
    [ search index=snow earliest=-24h@h latest=now sourcetype=snow:incident
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(u_service,u_cmdb_ci,business_service,"snow_svc")))
      | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(assignment_group,"snow_queue")))
      | eval create_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(sys_created_on,opened_at,"1970-01-01 00:00:00"))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval resolve_ts=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(resolved_at,closed_at,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval _time=coalesce(create_ts,now())
      | eval mttr_min=round(if(isnotnull(resolve_ts) AND isnotnull(create_ts) AND resolve_ts>=create_ts,(resolve_ts-create_ts)/60,0),2)
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats avg(mttr_min) AS mttr_min count AS snow_n BY _time service owner_team
      | eval episodes_per_hour=round(snow_n,3)
      | eval mtta_min=0.0
      | eval autoclose_pct=0.0
      | eval manualclose_pct=0.0
      | eval escalation_latency_p95_min=0.0
      | eval noise_ratio=0.0
      | eval repeated_episode_rate=0.0
      | eval lane="arm_snow" ]
| eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unspecified_service")))
| eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_team")))
| stats max(episodes_per_hour) AS episodes_per_hour max(mtta_min) AS mtta_min max(mttr_min) AS mttr_min max(autoclose_pct) AS autoclose_pct max(manualclose_pct) AS manualclose_pct max(escalation_latency_p95_min) AS escalation_latency_p95_min max(noise_ratio) AS noise_ratio max(repeated_episode_rate) AS repeated_episode_rate values(lane) AS lane_vals BY _time service owner_team
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv
     | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_title,"")))
     | eval owner_team_inv=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,"platform_ops")))
     | eval business_tier=trim(toString(coalesce(business_tier,tier,"standard")))
     | eval on_call_team=trim(toString(coalesce(on_call_team,pager_office,"primary_rotation")))
     | eval runbook_url=trim(toString(coalesce(runbook_url,"https://runbooks.example/itsi_episodes")))
     | fields service owner_team_inv business_tier on_call_team runbook_url ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team_inv,owner_team,"unowned_team")
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service,ci_name,"")))
     | dedup service
     | eval environment=trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"prod")))
     | fields service environment ]
| eval business_tier=coalesce(business_tier,"standard")
| eval on_call_team=coalesce(on_call_team,owner_team)
| sort 0 service - _time
| streamstats window=168 current=t global=f avg(mttr_min) AS mttr_7d_avg avg(mtta_min) AS mtta_7d_avg BY service owner_team
| eventstats perc95(mttr_min) AS mttr_env_p95 BY environment
| eval subgroup_mttr_anomaly=if(mttr_min>(coalesce(mttr_env_p95,0)*1.35) OR mttr_min>coalesce(mttr_7d_avg*1.5,999999),1,0)
| eval severity=case(coalesce(escalation_latency_p95_min,0)>15 OR coalesce(mttr_env_p95,0)>60 OR (coalesce(autoclose_pct,0)>40 AND coalesce(manualclose_pct,0)<12 AND subgroup_mttr_anomaly==1),"critical", coalesce(subgroup_mttr_anomaly,0)==1 OR coalesce(mttr_min,0)>60,"high", coalesce(autoclose_pct,0)>40,"high", coalesce(noise_ratio,0)>0.35 OR coalesce(repeated_episode_rate,0)>0.25,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","page_service_owner_review_neap_autoclose_escalation_ladder_audit_kv_and_ticketing_fidelity", severity=="high","triage_merged_episodes_suppression_rules_severity_promotion_and_api_throttle_illusion", severity=="medium","tune_smart_group_similarity_on_call_routing_and_service_tag_governance", true(),"continue_weekly_outcome_review_sliding_7d_and_sustained_mttr_retro")
| table _time, service, owner_team, business_tier, on_call_team, episodes_per_hour, mtta_min, mttr_min, autoclose_pct, manualclose_pct, escalation_latency_p95_min, noise_ratio, severity, recommended_action
```

Step 4 — Validate

Begin in a lab tenant. Create a synthetic episode that moves from open to acknowledged to resolved with known timestamps, confirm audit_kv or _audit extracts mtta_min and mttr_min consistent with stopwatch tests, and verify Splunk rows match Episode Review for the same episode_id.

Induce a short burst of related notables that should aggregate, confirm grouped and tracked arms move together within the same hourly bin, and verify noise_ratio responds when you deliberately close episodes without intermediate workflow states under change control.

Validate on-call arm by triggering a test page with a known routing key mapped in service inventory; confirm escalation_latency_p95_min stays below policy targets when schedules are healthy.

Validate ServiceNow arm by opening and resolving a non-production incident with timestamps; confirm mttr_min aligns within rounding tolerance.

Validate enrichment joins by running inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv alone and confirming service coverage for tier-zero paths. Run the cmdb subsearch alone and confirm dedup service returns one row per configuration item.

Validate anomaly logic by replaying a historical week where one squad had sustained MTTR elevation; confirm subgroup_mttr_anomaly flags appear before executives ask for the retrospective.

Negative validation: after recovery, when escalation_latency_p95_min is within target, auto-close share is balanced, and noise_ratio is low, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for canary services.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Night-shift rotation lengthens acknowledgement latency on high severity because the ladder lists a daytime roster first; fix schedule gaps, verify holiday overrides, and replay escalation tests before blaming applications.

Case 2 — Aggregation policy auto-closes live incidents while customer impact continues; disable aggressive auto-close, require manual verification for high severity, and compare ITSI closure timestamps to on-call pages.

Case 3 — Bot or auto-resolver integrations flap episodes closed and reopened; tighten action filters, require human acknowledgement for closure on revenue services, and correlate with correlation search logs from UC-13.2.6 when investigations demand engine detail.

Case 4 — Downstream ticketing application programming interface throttles at four hundred twenty-nine responses; MTTR looks stellar in ITSI while tickets lag; throttle-aware queues and idempotent updates restore fidelity.

Case 5 — Severity promotion rules misbehave after a content pack import; replay severity_change audits, restore templates, and validate NEAP escalation matrices against the new severity ladder.

Case 6 — Merged episodes hide individual MTTR for child services; drill split events, attribute time to originating KPI, and adjust smart group fields when merges obscure accountability.

Case 7 — On-call ladder skipped due to misconfigured routing keys; remap service inventory on_call_team fields, validate Splunk On-Call and PagerDuty service identifiers, and test page paths monthly.

Case 8 — Orphan episodes appear after rules-engine restart; compare tracked versus grouped counts, clear stuck dispatch queues per UC-13.2.6 guidance, and replay integrity dashboards.

Case 9 — Episodes never open due to suppression rule mismatch with live notables; reconcile suppression predicates with maintenance calendars and document intentional silence.

Case 10 — Misclassified service tags route metrics to the wrong owner_team; fix configuration management database alignment and backfill inventory CSV before judging squad performance.

Case 11 — Planned drill or game-day traffic mis-tagged as production; tag drill metadata, exclude rehearsal rows from executive scorecards, and preserve raw logs for auditors.

Case 12 — Cross-region timezone offsets misalign hourly bins; standardize time zone on forwarders and search heads, rebuild bins on coordinated universal time, and revalidate acknowledgement math.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists fourteen columns including business_tier and on_call_team; value differs from description; detailedImplementation contains no bold markdown markers.
visualization: Episode Review queue tables with MTTA and MTTR overlays, hourly outcome tiles by business_tier, on-call escalation sparklines, auto-close versus manual-close stacked bars, noise_ratio heatmaps, and drilldowns to aggregation policies and ServiceNow assignment groups.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.severity) AS alert_sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS service
| join type=left max=0 service [| tstats summariesonly=true avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS service ]
| table service alert_sev cpu_load
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts summaries when ITSI notables map into Alerts.dest for service keys; accelerate Performance summaries for hosts backing ITSI entities when correlating saturation to slow acknowledgement; retain at least thirty days for quarterly outcome retrospectives.

references.title: Splunk Docs — ITSI Event Analytics overview
Splunk Docs — Review episodes in ITSI
Splunk Docs — Configure Notable Event Aggregation Policies
Google SRE Book — Monitoring distributed systems
Atlassian — Incident metrics including MTTA and MTTR
Splunk Docs — Use the Episode Analytics dashboard

knownFalsePositives: Planned chaos engineering drills and game-day simulations can spike episode creation and acknowledgement latency without customer risk; tag and filter rehearsal metadata before executive reviews. Holiday on-call hand-offs legitimately slow acknowledgement when rotations are thin; compare against on-call schedule coverage rather than application regressions alone. Change-management freeze windows sometimes intentionally suppress auto-close actions; expect manual-close dominance and document the freeze ticket beside metrics. After-hours batch operations may generate benign notables that auto-close quickly; pair noise_ratio with business context before accusing teams of rubber-stamping closures. Training cohort onboarding sessions in shared tenants can inflate episode volume; use sandbox partitions or training labels to keep outcome metrics honest. Quarterly daylight-saving shifts can skew hourly bins for one day; widen tolerance windows when validating acknowledgement math. Vendor maintenance on ticketing APIs can delay ServiceNow timestamps while ITSI shows resolution; correlate external vendor status before blaming internal squads. Content-pack imports may temporarily widen NEAP auto-close windows; delay severity upgrades until policies stabilize. Executive dashboards that average MTTR across all severities can hide critical-tier outliers; insist on subgroup views when investigating anomalies. Mis-synchronized laptop clocks on analyst workstations can cause misleading manual timestamps in notes even when server-side audit times are correct.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads running ITSI with indexer clusters retaining alert indexes, typically on Linux or Kubernetes-hosted Splunk containers, integrated with SaaS on-call and ServiceNow endpoints.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.1 Service Health Score regression detection, UC-13.2.2 per-KPI degradation burn-rate alerting, UC-13.2.6 rules-engine health in isolation, generic Enterprise Security correlation except where NEAP objects are intentionally shared, or installation procedures for Splunk On-Call and ServiceNow connectors.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab ITSI tenant, create synthetic episodes with known acknowledgement and resolution delays, inject matching Splunk On-Call test pages and ServiceNow non-production incidents, then confirm uc_13_2_3_itsi_episode_outcome_effectiveness returns severity high or critical within two hourly runs with non-null recommended_action and enriched business_tier when inventory rows exist.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After clearing synthetic load and restoring healthy escalation latency below fifteen minutes with balanced auto-close share and low noise_ratio, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for canary services under test for four consecutive runs.

evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_3_itsi_episode_outcome_effectiveness with hourly schedule, exported CSV of alert rows joined to itsi_service_inventory.csv, and ticketed screenshots from Episode Review, Splunk On-Call or PagerDuty, and ServiceNow for the same service hour.
